title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself poses minimal direct risk, but its widespread adoption could lead to potential misuse by malicious actors to identify unpatched vulnerabilities in systems. Additionally, reliance on the tool without proper validation of its results may lead to incomplete or false security assessments.
recommended_actions: Evaluate the tool in a controlled environment to ensure its accuracy and reliability before deploying it in production.
Educate teams on the importance of validating vulnerability scan results and cross-referencing with other trusted sources.
Monitor for any updates or patches to the tool to address potential security flaws or misuse.

title: A permissive list of allowed inputs in ASUS Armoury Crate allows a local administrator to perform...
description:
A permissive list of allowed inputs in ASUS Armoury Crate allows a local administrator to perform arbitrary memory read/write operations or cause a system crash (BSOD) by bypassing the validation mechanism.Refer to the '
Security Update for Armoury Crate App ' section on the ASUS Security Advisory for more information.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in ASUS Armoury Crate allows a local administrator to exploit a permissive input validation mechanism, enabling arbitrary memory read/write operations or causing a system crash (BSOD). This could lead to privilege escalation, data corruption, or system instability.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply the latest security update for ASUS Armoury Crate as provided in the ASUS Security Advisory.
Restrict local administrator access to trusted personnel and monitor for unusual activity on systems running Armoury Crate.
Implement endpoint protection solutions to detect and mitigate exploit attempts targeting this vulnerability.

title: A vulnerability has been found in coollabsio coolify 4.0.0. Impacted is an unknown function of...
description: A vulnerability has been found in coollabsio coolify 4.0.0. Impacted is an unknown function of the component Image Name Handler. Such manipulation leads to os command injection. The attack may be performed from remote. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way. The changelog for 4.1.2 mentions "[i]mproved image, branch, proxy, and deployment input validation".

title: A weakness has been identified in kortix-ai suna up to 0.8.38. Affected by this issue is the...
description: A weakness has been identified in kortix-ai suna up to 0.8.38. Affected by this issue is the function router.replace/router.push of the file apps/frontend/src/app/auth/page.tsx of the component Auth Endpoint. Executing a manipulation of the argument returnURL can lead to cross site scripting. The attack may be launched remotely. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks. Upgrading to version 0.8.39 can resolve this issue. This patch is called f5dec7aa0c1b8fa

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The identified vulnerability in kortix-ai suna up to version 0.8.38 allows for cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks via the manipulation of the returnURL argument in the Auth Endpoint. This could lead to unauthorized script execution, potentially exposing sensitive user data or enabling phishing attacks.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to kortix-ai suna version 0.8.39, which includes the patch (f5dec7aa0c1b8fa) to resolve this issue.
Conduct a thorough review of application logs to identify any signs of exploitation of this vulnerability.
Implement input validation and sanitization for the returnURL parameter to prevent malicious payloads.

title: A security vulnerability has been detected in Radware Cyber Controller up to 10.11.0. This...
description: A security vulnerability has been detected in Radware Cyber Controller up to 10.11.0. This affects an unknown part of the component HTML Report Generation. The manipulation leads to HTML injection. Remote exploitation of the attack is possible. The exploit has been disclosed publicly and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Radware Cyber Controller up to version 10.11.0 allows for HTML injection through the HTML Report Generation component. This could lead to unauthorized content being injected into reports, potentially enabling phishing attacks, data manipulation, or other malicious activities. The public disclosure of the exploit increases the likelihood of exploitation.
recommended_actions: Immediately update Radware Cyber Controller to the latest version if available, or apply any patches/workarounds provided by the vendor.
Implement input validation and sanitization on the HTML Report Generation component to mitigate the risk of HTML injection.
Monitor systems for unusual activity or signs of exploitation, and review access logs for suspicious behavior.

description: A security flaw has been discovered in Edimax BR-6478AC V2 1.23. Affected by this vulnerability is the function mp of the file /goform/mp of the component POST Request Handler. Performing a manipulation of the argument command results in command injection. The attack may be initiated remotely. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows remote attackers to perform command injection via a manipulated POST request, potentially leading to unauthorized control of the device. This could result in data exfiltration, network compromise, or further attacks on connected systems.
recommended_actions: Immediately disable remote management on the Edimax BR-6478AC V2 router to reduce exposure to remote exploitation.
Replace or update the affected router with a more secure model or firmware if the vendor does not provide a patch.
Monitor network traffic for unusual activity and implement network segmentation to limit potential lateral movement.

title: A vulnerability was detected in lemonldap-ng up to 2.23.0. Impacted is an unknown function in the...

Impacted is an unknown function in the library lemonldap-ng-portal/lib/Lemonldap/NG/Portal/CDC.pm of the component SAML Common Domain Cookie Endpoint. Performing a manipulation of the argument url results in open redirect. The attack is possible to be carried out remotely. The exploit is now public and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to perform open redirect attacks, which can be used to redirect users to malicious websites, potentially leading to phishing or malware distribution. The exploit being public increases the likelihood of misuse.
recommended_actions: Upgrade lemonldap-ng to a version newer than 2.23.0 as soon as a patch is available.
Implement input validation and sanitization for the 'url' parameter to prevent exploitation.
Monitor for unusual redirection patterns and review logs for signs of exploitation.

title: libexpat before 2.8.2 does not consider XML_TOK_DATA_CHARS in doCdataSection and thus lacks...
description: libexpat before 2.8.2 does not consider XML_TOK_DATA_CHARS in doCdataSection and thus lacks handler call depth tracking for various calls from within handlers in cases of a policy violation. Thus, a use-after-free can occur. NOTE: this issue exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2026-50219.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-56412.

title: xmlwf in libexpat before 2.8.2 has an integer overflow for the output filename when -d outputDir...
description: xmlwf in libexpat before 2.8.2 has an integer overflow for the output filename when -d outputDir is used.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The integer overflow vulnerability in libexpat could allow attackers to manipulate or corrupt file paths when the -d outputDir option is used, potentially leading to data corruption, denial of service, or unauthorized access to sensitive files.
recommended_actions: Upgrade libexpat to version 2.8.2 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Audit systems and applications using libexpat to identify potential exploitation risks.
Implement input validation and sanitization for file paths to mitigate exploitation attempts.

title: CVE-2026-12211 - A flaw has been found in Intelbras iNVU 7016 FT 3.004.00IB000.0.T Build 2025-09-26. This impacts an
description: A flaw has been found in Intelbras iNVU 7016 FT 3.004.00IB000.0.T Build 2025-09-26. This impacts an unknown function of the file /RPC2_Loadfile/syslog/ of the component Web Interface. Executing a manipulation can lead to path traversal. The attack can be launched remotely. The exploit has been published and may be used. It is recommended to upgrade the affected component. The vendor was contacted early, responded in a very professional manner and quickly released a fixed version of the affected product.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows for path traversal attacks via the web interface of the Intelbras iNVU 7016 FT device. This could potentially lead to unauthorized access to sensitive files or directories on the device, compromising its integrity and confidentiality. However, the severity is rated as low due to the availability of a vendor patch and the limited scope of the vulnerability.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade the Intelbras iNVU 7016 FT firmware to the latest version provided by the vendor.
Restrict access to the device's web interface to trusted IP addresses using firewalls or network access controls.
Monitor logs for any suspicious activity related to the /RPC2_Loadfile/syslog/ endpoint and implement intrusion detection systems (IDS) to detect potential exploitation attempts.

title: CVE-2026-12209 - A security vulnerability has been detected in RubyLouvre avalon up to 2.2.10. The impacted element i
description: A security vulnerability has been detected in RubyLouvre avalon up to 2.2.10. The impacted element is an unknown function of the file src/filters/index.js of the component Template Filter Handler. Such manipulation leads to improperly controlled modification of object prototype attributes. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. The exploit has been disclosed publicly and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in RubyLouvre avalon up to version 2.2.10 allows attackers to exploit improperly controlled modifications of object prototype attributes via a remote attack. This could lead to unauthorized code execution, data manipulation, or other malicious activities, depending on how the library is implemented in applications.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade RubyLouvre avalon to a version beyond 2.2.10, if available. If no patch exists, consider replacing the library with a secure alternative.
Implement input validation and sanitization mechanisms to mitigate potential exploitation of the vulnerability.
Monitor public vulnerability databases and threat intelligence feeds for updates or proof-of-concept exploits related to CVE-2026-12209.

This product follows a rolling release approach for continuous delivery, so version details for affected or updated releases are not provided. The patch is named 91a31aac1b0f4dbc6b8bef9f6eff0b7912e0bc65. Applying a patch is the recommended action to fix this issue. The vendor confirms: "Research export endpoints now require an authenticated agent with the research_exports capability".
